info.title: Rapidata Identity API
info.description: The API for the Rapidata Identity service

get.summary: Lists the grant tuples on a capability, one cursor page at a time.
get.description: Covers both organization-level and direct customer grants. Follow nextCursor to page.

parameters.name: capability
parameters.description: The capability to inspect.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: The opaque cursor from the previous page; omit for the first page.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: The number of items per page.

get.summary: Lists the capabilities a customer holds.
get.description: capabilities are grants held through organization membership.

parameters.name: customerId
parameters.description: The customer to inspect.

get.summary: Lists the capabilities granted to an organization.

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: The organization to inspect.

put.summary: Grants a capability to an organization.
put.description: Idempotent: re-granting an existing grant is a no-op.

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: The organization to grant the capability to.

parameters.name: capability
parameters.description: The capability to grant.

delete.description: Idempotent: revoking a grant that does not exist is a no-op.

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: The organization to revoke the capability from.

parameters.name: capability
parameters.description: The capability to revoke.

post.summary: Creates a new client for the current customer.

parameters.name: clientId
parameters.description: The id of the client to delete.

get.summary: Gets the details of a specific client.

parameters.name: clientId
parameters.description: The id of the client to retrieve.

get.summary: Queries clients for the current customer.
get.description: Clients are service credentials that allow automated processes to authenticate against the API.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The 1-based page index.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: The number of items per page.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort fields. Prefix with - for descending order (e.g. -created_at).

parameters.name: client_id
parameters.description: Filter by client_id.

parameters.name: display_name
parameters.description: Filter by display_name.

parameters.name: created_at
parameters.description: Filter by created_at.

parameters.name: logic
parameters.description: How to combine the field filters: "and" (default) requires every filter to match, "or" requires any of them to match.

post.summary: Registers a new client using the OpenID Connect Dynamic Client Registration protocol.

get.summary: Queries customers with filtering and pagination.
get.description: Results are scoped to customers that belong to the caller's organization.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The 1-based page index.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: The number of items per page.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort fields. Prefix with - for descending order (e.g. -created_at).

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Filter by id.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Filter by email.

parameters.name: organization_name
parameters.description: Filter by organization_name.

parameters.name: organization_id
parameters.description: Filter by organization_id.

parameters.name: logic
parameters.description: How to combine the field filters: "and" (default) requires every filter to match, "or" requires any of them to match.

post.summary: Creates a pair of read and write bridge token keys for a client.
post.description: The write key stores the authentication result and the read key retrieves it.

parameters.name: clientId
parameters.description: The client ID to create the keys for.

get.summary: Reads the bridge token keys for a given read key.
get.description: Returns 200 with the keys when available, 202 when they are not yet available, or 404 when not found.

parameters.name: readKey
parameters.description: The read key to read the bridge token keys for.

post.summary: Signs in a user using a token received from Google One Tap.
post.description: Redirects to the register confirmation page when the account requires email confirmation.

requestBody.description: The body of the request containing the id token.

post.summary: Stores the referrer in a cookie for later use during sign-up.

parameters.name: referrer
parameters.description: The referrer value to store.

post.summary: Signs an email address up to the newsletter.

requestBody.description: The email to subscribe and a recaptcha token.

post.summary: Removes an email address from the newsletter.

requestBody.description: The email to unsubscribe and a recaptcha token.

post.summary: Accepts the invitation identified by the token.
post.description:
Responds with 409 when the invitation is expired, already used, or was issued to a different
 email than the calling customer's.

put.summary: Sets the given member's org-scoped role.
put.description:
Responds with 404 when the customer is not a member and 409 when changing the role of the
 organization's last owner.

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: The id of the organization.

parameters.name: customerId
parameters.description: The id of the member whose role changes.

post.summary: Creates an organization and makes the calling customer its owner.
post.description:
The calling customer becomes both the owner and the billing customer. Responds with 400 when
 the slug is already taken.

get.summary: Returns a paged list of the caller's organizations, each with the caller's role.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The 1-based page index.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: The number of items per page.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort fields. Prefix with - for descending order (e.g. -created_at).

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Filter by name.

parameters.name: slug
parameters.description: Filter by slug.

parameters.name: logic
parameters.description: How to combine the field filters: "and" (default) requires every filter to match, "or" requires any of them to match.

get.summary: Returns the organization with the given id.

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: The id of the organization to retrieve.

patch.summary: Updates the given organization's provided fields.
patch.description:
Only the fields present in the body are changed. Responds with 400 when the slug is already
 taken by another organization.

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: The id of the organization to update.

post.summary: Creates an invitation to join the organization.
post.description: Returns a single-use token the invitee presents to the accept-invitation endpoint.

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: The id of the organization to invite into.

get.summary: Returns the pending invitations of the given organization.

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: The id of the organization whose invitations to list.

get.summary: Returns the members of the given organization.

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: The id of the organization whose members to list.

get.summary: Returns a paged list of every organization.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The 1-based page index.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: The number of items per page.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort fields. Prefix with - for descending order (e.g. -created_at).

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Filter by name.

parameters.name: slug
parameters.description: Filter by slug.

parameters.name: logic
parameters.description: How to combine the field filters: "and" (default) requires every filter to match, "or" requires any of them to match.

delete.summary: Removes the given customer's membership from the organization.
delete.description:
Responds with 404 when the customer is not a member and 409 when removing the organization's
 last owner.

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: The id of the organization.

parameters.name: customerId
parameters.description: The id of the customer to remove.

delete.summary: Revokes the given invitation so its token can no longer be accepted.
delete.description:
Responds with 404 when the invitation does not belong to the organization and 409 when it is
 not pending.

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: The id of the organization.

parameters.name: invitationId
parameters.description: The id of the invitation to revoke.

put.summary: Sets the support Slack channel URL for the organization owned by the given customer.
put.description: A null or empty URL clears the channel. Responds with 404 when no organization is owned by the given customer.

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The customer ID that owns the organization.

requestBody.description: The support Slack channel URL to set or clear.

put.summary: Switches the calling customer's active organization to the given one.
put.description:
The active organization determines the token's org claim; the change takes effect the next time
 the client refreshes its token. Responds with 403 when the caller is not a member of the target
 organization.

post.summary: Submits a survey for the calling customer.

membershipId.description: The id of the created membership.

Capability.description:
The named platform-wide capability entitlements a customer can be granted. Each maps to a
`capability` object in the authorization graph; convert to its object id with
string CapabilityExtensions.ToObjectId(Capability capability). Kept as a typed enum so
capability grants read explicitly at call sites (and, later, on endpoint contracts) instead of
passing raw id strings around.

role.description: The org-scoped role to assign to the member.

scope.description:
String containing a space-separated list of scope values
 that the client can use when requesting access tokens.

contacts.description:
Array of strings representing ways to contact people responsible for this client,
 typically email addresses.

Client.description:
The response containing the id and secret of a dynamically registered client.
 Additionally, it contains all registered metadata for the client.

readKey.description: The read key used to retrieve the authentication result.

writeKey.description: The write key used to store the authentication result.

displayName.description: An optional display name for the client.

clientId.description: The id of the created client.

clientSecret.description: The secret of the created client.

name.description: The human-readable name of the organization.

slug.description: A URL-safe unique identifier for the organization.

primaryDomain.description: The primary email domain associated with the organization, if any.

organizationId.description: The id of the created organization.

scope.description:
String containing a space-separated list of scope values
 that the client can use when requesting access tokens.

contacts.description:
Array of strings representing ways to contact people responsible for this client,
 typically email addresses.

jwks.description: Client's JSON Web Key Set [RFC7517] document value, which contains the client's public keys.

DynamicClientRegistrationRequest.description: The request body for dynamic client registration.

GetCapabilityGranteesEndpoint_CursorPage.description:
A page of results identified by an opaque cursor rather than an offset. Used where the
underlying store pages by continuation token and a total count is unavailable or expensive.
string? CursorPagedResult<T>.NextCursor is null on the last page.

capabilities.description: Capabilities granted to the customer through organization membership.
The capabilities granted to the organization, held by all of its members.

name.description: The human-readable name of the organization.

slug.description: The URL-safe unique identifier of the organization.

billingCustomerId.description: The customer that owns billing for the organization.

primaryDomain.description: The primary email domain associated with the organization, if any.

idToken.description: The id token received from the Google One Tap login.

requiresEmailConfirmation.description: Whether the account still requires email confirmation.

email.description: The email of the signed-in user, if available.

role.description: The org-scoped role the resulting membership will confer.

invitationId.description: The id of the created invitation.

token.description: The single-use token used to accept the invitation.

expiresAt.description: The instant after which the invitation can no longer be accepted.

invitations.description: The pending invitations of the organization.

name.description: The human-readable name of the organization.

slug.description: The URL-safe unique identifier of the organization.

memberCount.description: The number of members the organization has.

displayName.description: The optional display name of the client.

createdAt.description: The time at which the client was created.

id.description: The unique identifier of the customer.

email.description: The email address of the customer.

organizationName.description: The name of the organization this customer belongs to, if any.

organizationId.description: The identifier of the organization this customer belongs to, if any.

name.description: The human-readable name of the organization.

slug.description: The URL-safe unique identifier of the organization.

role.description: The caller's role in the organization.

expiresIn.description: The lifetime of the access token, in seconds.

refreshToken.description: The refresh token used to obtain new access tokens.

tokenType.description: The type of the issued token.

scope.description: The scopes granted to the issued token.

message.description: A message indicating that the keys are not yet available.

url.description: The support Slack channel URL to set, or null/empty to clear it.

email.description: The email of the user to subscribe.

token.description: The recaptcha token used to verify the caller is not a bot.

organizationId.description: The id of the organization to activate.

email.description: The email of the user to unsubscribe.

token.description: The recaptcha token used to verify the caller is not a bot.

name.description: The new human-readable name, if changing it.

slug.description: The new URL-safe unique identifier, if changing it.
